RequirementInformationc
PipProvidera
Pip's provider implementation for resolvelib.
    :params constraints: A mapping of constraints specified by the user. Keys
        are canonicalized project names.
    :params ignore_dependencies: Whether the user specified ``--no-deps``.
    :params upgrade_strategy: The user-specified upgrade strategy.
    :params user_requested: A set of canonicalized package names that the user
        supplied for pip to install/upgrade.
    N)
factory
constraints
ignore_dependencies
upgrade_strategy
user_requested
returnc
math
P/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pip/_internal/resolution/resolvelib/provider.py
<lambda>@
z&PipProvider.__init__.<locals>.<lambda>)

_known_depths)
selfr
__init__3
PipProvider.__init__)
requirement_or_candidater
name)
identifyB
PipProvider.identify
PreferenceInformationr
identifier
resolutions
candidates
information
backtrack_causesr
Produce a sort key for given requirement based on preference.
        The lower the return value is, the more preferred this group of
        arguments is.
        Currently pip considers the followings in order:
        * Prefer if any of the known requirements is "direct", e.g. points to an
          explicit URL.
        * If equal, prefer if any requirement is "pinned", i.e. contains
          operator ``===`` or ``==``.
        * If equal, calculate an approximate "depth" and resolve requirements
          closer to the user-specified requirements first.
        * Order user-specified requirements by the order they are specified.
        * If equal, prefers "non-free" requirements, i.e. contains at least one
          operator, such as ``>=`` or ``<``.
        * If equal, order alphabetically for consistency (helps debuggability).
        c
get_candidate_lookup)
	<genexpr>_
z-PipProvider.get_preference.<locals>.<genexpr>c
operator)
specifier_set
	specifierr
<listcomp>a
z.PipProvider.get_preference.<locals>.<listcomp>c
ireqr

minr'

	candidateZ
ireqsZ	operatorsZ

get_preferenceE
PipProvider.get_preference)
	partitionr

open_bracketr6
_get_constraint
PipProvider._get_constraint)
requirements
incompatibilitiesr
Are upgrades allowed for this project?
            This checks the upgrade strategy, and whether the project was one
            that the user specified in the command line, in order to decide
            whether we should upgrade if there's a newer version available.
            (Note that we don't need access to the `--upgrade` flag, because
            an upgrade strategy of "to-satisfy-only" means that `--upgrade`
            was not specified).
            Z
eagerTz
only-if-neededF)

z7PipProvider.find_matches.<locals>._eligible_for_upgrade)
